evals.prompt: Build an orders route component that lists orders in a data table. Columns: Order ID, Customer, Status (badge), Total, Date. Use the Admin design system.
evals.expected_output: A React component that imports DataTable and Badge from ~/ui, defines a typed ColumnDef<T> array, renders <DataTable columns={...} items={...} />, and uses Badge variants for status semantics.
expectations: Uses ColumnDef<T> type annotation for column definitions
Status column uses <Badge variant='...'> instead of a custom span
Uses React className where custom classes are needed

evals.prompt: Add a country/region selector to a customer address form. It should be a dropdown with a label and error state support. Use the Admin design system.
evals.expected_output: Uses modern Select namespace components with semantic label and error text, with imports from ~/ui.
expectations: Uses semantic label and error text connected to the control
Does not implement custom dropdown accessibility

evals.prompt: Create a form section for editing a customer's profile with fields for name and bio. Include labels and error state support. Use the Admin design system.
evals.expected_output: Uses Input for the name field, a native textarea for bio, and semantic labels/descriptions/error text.
expectations: Uses semantic labels for each form field
Uses modern Input for the text field
Uses a native textarea for the multi-line field because no modern Textarea exists yet
Connects error text to validation state

evals.prompt: Add a small status indicator badge next to an order title that shows Paid, Pending, or Refunded. Use the Admin design system.
evals.expected_output: Uses modern Badge component with an appropriate semantic variant.
expectations: Uses Badge from the design system
Uses variant prop rather than custom color classes
